title: cascarilla pavonii
intro: Cascarilla pavonii, also known as Sweet Woodruff or Cassina, is a flowering plant native to Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cascarilla pavonii include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Cascarilla pavonii can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
